qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: GraphQL addComment and upVote/downVote mutations bypass component-level authorization
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: Does not match the advisory root cause: the advisory describes stored XSS from the user name field rendered on comment pages, while this finding concerns missing component-level authorization checks in GraphQL comment and vote mutations.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described Decidim stored XSS in the user name field or the equivalent rendering/escaping root cause on comment pages. The only qualifying finding is an open-world GraphQL authorization bypass affecting addComment and vote mutations.
evidence: Advisory GHSA-fc46-r95f-hq7g describes stored code execution/XSS through the user name field when a victim passively visits a comment page.
graphql_comment_auth_bypass/vulnerability_report.json describes missing enforce_permission_to checks in Decidim GraphQL addComment, upVote, and downVote mutation paths.

The finding's cited files are Decidim comments GraphQL mutation, vote resolver, controller, permission, and component authorization code; they do not identify a user-name sanitizer, renderer, escaping bypass, or stored XSS source-to-sink path.
